engineIntelligence.h2: Porsche Cayman Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Porsche Cayman in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Porsche Cayman M97.21 engine - petrol 3.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: IMS bearing wear - intermediate shaft bearing can fail between 60k-100k miles on pre-2009 units.
Bore scoring - cylinder wall scoring from piston slap affects high-mileage M97 engines.
Air-oil separator failure - PCV system degradation causes oil consumption and rough idle.

cards.altText: Porsche Cayman MA1.22 engine - petrol 2.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits affect performance after 50k miles.
Timing chain tensioner wear - early MA1 units may experience chain rattle on cold starts.

cards.altText: Porsche Cayman DFB engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger wastegate rattle - VTG actuator wear causes boost inconsistency on higher-mileage units.
Oil dilution from short journeys - fuel washdown can affect longevity in urban-driven examples.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £5,500-£11,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable 718 turbo units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Porsche Cayman Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 2.7L Petrol
entries.history: Base Cayman engine; VarioCam Plus valve timing, prone to IMS bearing concerns pre-2009.
commonFailures: IMS bearing wear - intermediate shaft bearing failure risk 60k-100k miles
Bore scoring - cylinder wall damage from piston slap on high-mileage units
Air-oil separator degradation - causes oil consumption and rough running

entries.title: 3.4L Petrol
entries.history: Cayman S flagship; derived from 997 Carrera, higher output with VarioCam Plus.
compatibleVariants: Cayman S, Cayman S Tiptronic, Cayman S Sport
commonFailures: IMS bearing wear - intermediate shaft bearing can fail between 60k-100k miles
Bore scoring - cylinder wall scoring from piston slap affects high-mileage units
Timing chain tensioner wear - chain rattle on cold starts, especially pre-facelift

entries.title: 3.4L Petrol (Sport)
entries.history: Limited-run Cayman S Sport; free-flow exhaust, +8hp over standard M97.21.

Air-oil separator failure - PCV system degradation causes oil consumption

Water pump failure - plastic impeller wear causes coolant leaks
Variocam solenoid wear - variable timing issues on higher-mileage units

entries.title: 3.4L Petrol

commonFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits affect performance
Timing chain tensioner wear - early MA1 units may experience cold-start rattle
Rear main seal leak - oil seepage at gearbox interface on high-mileage cars

entries.history: 981-generation base engine; refined direct injection, improved efficiency.

Thermostat housing leak - coolant seepage at housing gasket

commonFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits require walnut blasting

commonFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup - intake valve deposits on high-mileage track cars
Oil cooler seal leak - seepage at oil cooler housing gasket

commonProblems.h2: Porsche Cayman Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Porsche Cayman's flat-six and turbo flat-four engines are generally robust, but pre-2009 M96/M97 units face IMS bearing and bore scoring risks, while later MA1 and D-series engines see direct injection carbon buildup and turbo wear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: IMS Bearing Failure - Why Pre-2009 Cayman Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Cayman, Cayman S (2006-2008, M97.20 & M97.21)
problems.rootCause: The intermediate shaft bearing's sealed design can lose lubrication, leading to bearing collapse and catastrophic engine damage if not addressed.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove gearbox, replace IMS bearing with upgraded ceramic unit, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; viable before bearing noise or metal debris appears

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace bearings, pistons, liners; address collateral damage
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught early before crank/web damage; extends life 50k+ miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with IMS solution, upgraded components, bench test, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or severely damaged units; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: On a Cayman worth £8,000-£15,000 with early IMS noise but no metal debris, a preventative bearing replacement offers excellent value. If metal is in the sump or knocking is present, a rebuilt engine with IMS solution is the only reliable long-term fix.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M97 engine prices for your Porsche Cayman

problems.h4: Bore Scoring Repair - Cylinder Wall Damage on M96/M97 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Porsche Cayman, Cayman S (2006-2008, M97.20 & M97.21)
problems.rootCause: Piston skirt design and thermal expansion can cause piston-to-cylinder contact, scoring the Nikasil-coated cylinder walls and leading to compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Borescope inspection, compression test, oil analysis to assess severity
repairOptions.longevity: Diagnostic only; suitable for early detection before major damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install Nickies or similar aluminium alloy sleeves, new pistons, rings
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent repair for scoring; suitable if crank/conrods are undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with sleeved block or replacement short block, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for severe scoring or when combined with other failures; peace of mind

problems.recommendation: If borescope shows light scoring with good compression, monitoring may suffice short-term. For visible scoring with compression loss, cylinder sleeving is cost-effective. If scoring is severe or combined with IMS failure, a rebuilt engine is the pragmatic choice.
problems.cta: Get quotes for bore scoring repair on your Porsche Cayman

problems.h4: Direct Injection Carbon Buildup - MA1 & D-Series Intake Valve Deposits
problems.rootCause: Direct injection systems spray fuel into the cylinder, not over the intake valves, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate and restrict airflow, causing rough idle and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast intake valves with crushed walnut shells, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative maintenance; recommended every 40k-50k miles on DI engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting plus intake manifold, throttle body, EGR cleaning, software reset
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive cleaning; suitable for noticeable power loss or rough running

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if carbon buildup caused valve damage or severe performance loss
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely needed for carbon alone; consider only if combined with other failures

problems.recommendation: Walnut blasting every 40k-50k miles is inexpensive preventative maintenance that preserves performance and fuel economy. If you're experiencing rough idle or power loss, start with a full induction service before considering major repairs.
problems.cta: Compare MA1 engine cleaning services for your Porsche Cayman

problems.h4: Turbocharger Wastegate Rattle - 718 Cayman DFB/DFC Engines
problems.affectedModels: Porsche 718 Cayman, Cayman S (2016-2024, DFB, DFC)
problems.rootCause: The variable turbine geometry (VTG) wastegate actuator can develop play, causing a metallic rattle on overrun and inconsistent boost pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Adjust wastegate linkage, recalibrate actuator, test boost response
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; suitable for early-stage rattle with no performance loss

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace wastegate actuator, bearings, seals, balance assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent repair for wastegate issues; restores boost consistency

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if turbo failure caused engine damage (rare)
repairOptions.longevity: Not typically needed for wastegate issues alone; consider if combined with oil starvation

problems.recommendation: Early wastegate rattle can often be adjusted or the actuator replaced independently. If boost pressure is affected or rattling is severe, a turbo rebuild is cost-effective. Engine replacement is rarely justified for turbo issues alone on the 718.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Cold-Start Rattle on Early MA1 Engines
problems.rootCause: Early MA1 tensioners can lose hydraulic pressure when cold, allowing chain slack and a brief rattle on startup; prolonged neglect risks chain jump or guide damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner with updated part, reset chain tension, test
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative; suitable for early rattle with no guide wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, chain, guide rails, sprockets; full timing service
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if guides show wear or rattle is persistent; restores quiet operation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if chain jump caused valve/piston contact (rare with early detection)
repairOptions.longevity: Not typically needed; consider only if catastrophic timing failure occurred

problems.recommendation: Address cold-start rattle promptly with a tensioner replacement. If guides are worn or rattle is severe, a full timing service prevents costly damage. Engine replacement is rarely justified unless valve contact occurred.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MA1 timing service on your Porsche Cayman

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Porsche Cayman
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing IMS bearing concerns on a 987, carbon buildup on a 981, or turbo wear on a 718, EngineMarket connects you with UK Porsche Cayman specialists who understand these engines. Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used engine prices, all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide supply & fit. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Porsche Cayman Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Porsche Cayman and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Porsche Cayman Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Porsche Cayman is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify your unit.

items.title: Porsche Cayman 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.7L flat-six powered the entry-level Cayman across 987 and 981 generations, offering balanced performance and efficiency with VarioCam Plus valve timing. It remains a popular choice for cost-conscious replacements.
commonFailurePoints: IMS bearing wear on M97 units; direct injection carbon buildup on MA1.22
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 2.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman 2.9L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced with the 987.2 facelift, the 2.9L direct-injection flat-six replaced the 2.7L for improved power and emissions, while eliminating the IMS bearing concern of earlier designs.
commonFailurePoints: Direct injection carbon buildup; water pump impeller wear
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 2.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman 3.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.4L flat-six defined the Cayman S across 987 and 981 generations, delivering strong performance with VarioCam Plus and, from 2009, direct injection for efficiency gains.
commonFailurePoints: bore scoring on M97; carbon buildup on MA1 variants
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 3.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman 3.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Reserved for the 981-generation GT4, the 3.8L naturally aspirated flat-six delivers track-focused performance with high-revving character and robust construction.
commonFailurePoints: Direct injection carbon buildup; oil cooler seal leaks under track use
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 3.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L flat-six powers the ultimate Cayman variants - GTS 4.0, GT4 and GT4 RS - offering naturally aspirated responsiveness and motorsport-derived durability.
commonFailurePoints: Direct injection carbon buildup; high-stress component wear on track-focused examples
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 718-generation base Cayman adopted a turbocharged 2.0L flat-four, delivering strong low-end torque and improved efficiency versus the outgoing naturally aspirated six.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo wastegate rattle; oil dilution from short journeys
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 718 Cayman S and early GTS use a larger 2.5L turbo flat-four, offering increased power and torque while retaining the efficiency benefits of downsized forced induction.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo wastegate rattle; intercooler condensation in humid climates
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Porsche Cayman Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: All Porsche Cayman variants use petrol engines, spanning naturally aspirated flat-six units (M96/M97/MA1) and turbocharged flat-four engines (DFB/DFC) across three generations.

engineYears.h2: Porsche Cayman Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Porsche Cayman's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The first Cayman launched with M97 flat-six engines, sharing architecture with the 997 911 but carrying IMS bearing and bore scoring risks.
keyChanges: Cayman nameplate introduced as fastback coupé variant of Boxster platform
M97.20 (2.7L) and M97.21 (3.4L) flat-six engines fitted
knownFor: IMS bearing failure risk on pre-2009 units
Bore scoring concerns on high-mileage M97 engines
Strong driving dynamics and balanced chassis
High enthusiast demand for replacement engines with IMS solutions
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2008 Porsche Cayman replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche Cayman IMS bearing replacement • Porsche Cayman bore scoring repair • M97.21 engine rebuild cost

years.preview: 987.2 Facelift: Direct Injection & IMS-Free MA1 Engines
years.description: The 987.2 facelift introduced direct-injection MA1 engines, eliminating the IMS bearing concern while improving power and efficiency.
knownFor: IMS-free design significantly improves long-term reliability
Direct injection carbon buildup becomes new maintenance consideration
Cayman R lightweight variant highly sought after
Strong UK specialist support for MA1 engine rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Porsche Cayman replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche Cayman MA1.21 engine replacement • Cayman R engine rebuild • Direct injection carbon cleaning cost

years.description: The all-new 981 platform brought refined MA1 engines with improved efficiency, plus the debut of the track-focused GT4 with a 3.8L variant.
mainEngines: MA1.23 - 3.4L petrol direct injection (Cayman S, GTS)
knownFor: Generally robust engines with carbon buildup as primary maintenance item
GT4's 3.8L engine highly regarded for track durability
Strong residual values drive demand for quality rebuilt units
Direct injection walnut blasting now routine maintenance item
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2016 Porsche Cayman replacement engines
years.ticker: Porsche Cayman GT4 engine replacement • MA1.23 carbon buildup service • 981 Cayman rebuild cost

years.preview: 718 Generation: Turbo Flat-Four & Return of NA 4.0L
years.description: The 718 rebranding introduced turbocharged flat-four engines for base/S models, while performance variants retained naturally aspirated flat-six power.
mainEngines: DFB - 2.0L turbo petrol flat-four (718 Cayman, Cayman T)
DFC - 2.5L turbo petrol flat-four (718 Cayman S, early GTS)
MA1.24 - 4.0L petrol flat-six (718 GTS 4.0, GT4, GT4 RS)
knownFor: Turbo wastegate rattle common on DFB/DFC engines at higher mileage
Oil dilution concerns on short-journey urban driving with turbo engines
Naturally aspirated 4.0L variants highly prized for track use
Strong specialist network for both turbo and NA engine rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2024 Porsche Cayman replacement engines
years.ticker: 718 Cayman turbo engine replacement • DFB wastegate repair • GT4 RS engine rebuild cost

faq.h2: Porsche Cayman Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Porsche Cayman engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Porsche Cayman engine typically costs £3,800-£11,200 supply-only, depending on engine code, variant and condition. Supply & fit adds £1,200-£2,800 labour.
keyPoints: Base M97/MA1 engines start around £3,800-£7,500 rebuilt
Cayman S and performance variants range £4,200-£9,500
718 turbo and GT4/GTS 4.0 engines £5,500-£15,800
Prices include upgraded components addressing known failures
All engines carry minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most reliable Porsche Cayman engine code?
items.answer: The MA1.21 (3.4L, 2009-2012) and MA1.23 (3.4L, 2013-2016) are widely regarded as the most reliable, being IMS-free with robust construction and strong specialist support.
keyPoints: MA1 series eliminated the IMS bearing failure risk of M96/M97
Direct injection requires periodic carbon cleaning but is manageable
MA1.21/23 benefit from mature specialist rebuild expertise
Regular servicing and warm-up routines extend longevity significantly
Avoid pre-2009 M97 units without documented IMS solution
items.cta: Get quotes for MA1.21 Porsche Cayman engine replacement

items.question: Can I upgrade my Porsche Cayman to a later engine code?
items.answer: Engine swaps are technically possible but complex and costly; compatibility, ECU coding, and emissions compliance make like-for-like replacement the pragmatic choice for most owners.
keyPoints: M97 to MA1 swap requires extensive wiring and ECU adaptation
981 to 718 turbo conversion is prohibitively complex and expensive
Emissions and MOT compliance must be verified for any swap
Specialist consultation essential before committing to upgrade path
Like-for-like rebuilt engine offers best value and reliability
items.cta: Speak to a Porsche Cayman engine specialist about compatibility

items.question: How long does a Porsche Cayman engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 3-7 working days; supply & fit installations take 2-4 days at a specialist workshop depending on complexity and parts availability.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines are often held in stock for common codes
Rare variants or performance engines may require build-to-order
Supply & fit includes removal, installation, adaptation and road test
Delays can occur if ancillary parts or gaskets need replacement
Book workshop slots in advance during peak seasons
items.cta: Find Porsche Cayman engine supply & fit availability

items.question: Do Porsche Cayman replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with extended options available on premium rebuilds.
keyPoints: Standard warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across the UK network
Extended warranties available on remanufactured and performance units
Warranty terms vary by specialist; confirm before ordering
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Porsche Cayman engine warranty options

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and remanufactured Porsche Cayman engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines replace wear items; rebuilt engines include upgrades addressing known failures; remanufactured engines meet factory specifications with all-new or reconditioned components.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: clean, inspect, replace gaskets/seals/wear items
Rebuilt: reconditioned plus upgraded IMS bearings, sleeves or turbo components
Remanufactured: factory-spec rebuild with all components machined or replaced
Rebuilt offers best value for addressing known Cayman failure points
Remanufactured ideal for collectors, track cars or maximum longevity
items.cta: View Porsche Cayman engine rebuild options

items.question: Will a replacement Porsche Cayman engine pass MOT emissions?
items.answer: Yes - engines supplied through EngineMarket are configured to meet original emissions specifications; ensure your specialist confirms catalytic converter and ECU compatibility for MOT compliance.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines retain original emissions hardware where possible
Direct injection engines require proper ECU adaptation for emissions
Catalytic converter condition affects MOT pass; replace if degraded
Specialist workshops can perform pre-MOT emissions checks
Keep documentation of engine code match for MOT tester reference
items.cta: Enquire about MOT-compliant Porsche Cayman engine supply

items.question: Can you supply a Porsche Cayman engine for export outside the UK?
items.answer: Yes - many specialists in our network offer international shipping; confirm export documentation, duties and warranty coverage before ordering for overseas delivery.
keyPoints: Export engines require customs documentation and compliance checks
Shipping costs and import duties vary by destination country
Warranty coverage may differ for international orders; confirm terms
Allow extra time for export paperwork and freight arrangements
Use a specialist experienced in Porsche engine exports for smooth process
items.cta: Enquire about Porsche Cayman engine export options

items.question: How do I find my Porsche Cayman engine code?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing or on the VIN plate; enter your registration above for instant DVLA verification of code and variant.
keyPoints: Engine code format: M97.21, MA1.22, DFB etc.
Locate stamp on block or check service records/V5C
DVLA lookup via registration provides instant code confirmation
Engine code determines compatible replacement options and pricing
Photograph the stamp when sourcing parts or quotes
items.cta: Use our Porsche Cayman engine code lookup tool

items.question: Should I repair my Porsche Cayman engine or replace it?
items.answer: For isolated issues like carbon buildup or turbo wastegate rattle, repair may suffice; for IMS failure, bore scoring or major mechanical damage, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
keyPoints: Diagnostic borescope and compression tests inform repair viability
IMS bearing or bore scoring damage usually warrants full replacement
Rebuilt engines include upgrades preventing recurrence of known failures
Consider vehicle value, mileage and ownership plans in decision
Compare repair quotes against rebuilt engine pricing with warranty
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for your Porsche Cayman engine decision
